> The most merciful thing in the world, I think, is the inability of the human mind to correlate all its contents. We live on a placid island of ignorance in the midst of black seas of infinity, and it was not meant that we should voyage far. The sciences, each straining in its own direction, have hitherto harmed us little; but some day the piecing together of dissociated knowledge will open up such terrifying vistas of reality, and of our frightful position therein, that we shall either go mad from the revelation or flee from the deadly light into the peace and safety of a new dark age.
People added to it like a collective world
still one of the best opening paragraphs in english literature

my favorite Mythos stories are not from Lovecraft lol
but reading Lovecraft really shows me how “oh, this really is the dawn of the genre”
and authors after him refined that
i do make an effort to try but i must admit it's easier to process the stories through other people narrating it instead of the original writing
i can appreciate the works for its themes and concepts
especially his interpretations of the unknown

his racism against people of color is also the reason for this <:takenotes:1357186646294138890> 
honestly when I read I feel like it’s not racism but instead deep rooted fear and mental illness
the way he described them like some unknown forces with unfathomable intentions really makes me feel like he’s afraid of them because he doesn’t know anything about them

honestly this is part of the reason why I like Wanda
she’s the antithesis of this
her concepts are mostly white women and they chose this Wanda

so
ghoul is originally a feminine word
back then before we have gothic depictions of ghoul
they’re the female witches or female killers that lure travelers on 🏜️ and eat them
also a term refer to graverobber but still female
you can see the deep rooted misogyny here
but when we have the first French translation of One Thounsand and One Nights
we are introduced to the concept of witches that works with evil corpse eating monster
so the term ghoul is no longer associated with female killers but now corpse eating monsters

you see this image
yeah they used Turkish for the words
so why Turkish here?
bc the French version is translated from the Turkish version

so they used some

creative freedom
